design_tokens.color: Light editorial base. --paper:#F7F5F0 (warm off-white), --paper-2:#EFEBE2 (section shift), --ink:#14130F (near-black warm), --ink-60:#4A473F, --ink-30:#8C887C. ONE accent: --gold:#C8912A with --gold-hi:#E6B24C and --gold-ink:#7A5510 for text-on-paper legibility (use gold-ink for accented words to keep AA contrast). Mascot/objects carry the literal metallic gold; UI accent stays the single flat gold. Danger/slop tone borrows --ink at low opacity + hatch texture, never red. Semantic: --line:rgba(20,19,15,.12), --lift:0 1px 0 rgba(20,19,15,.04). NO gradients-as-decoration except one controlled radial 'spotlight' behind mascot. Dark is used ONLY as an inverted anchor band (Section 4 'You own it') --ink background with --paper text — deliberate, once, for contrast rhythm.
design_tokens.type_scale_and_two_tone: Two families max. Display: a tight grotesque (e.g. 'Söhne Breit' fallback -apple-system/Inter-Tight) for headlines; Text: same-family regular for body; Mono ('JetBrains Mono') ONLY inside code/eval/handover chips. Fluid scale via clamp: --step--1 .82rem; --step-0 1rem; --step-1 1.25rem; --step-2 clamp(1.5,1.1rem+2vw,2.1rem); --step-3 clamp(2.2,1.4rem+4vw,3.4rem); --step-4 clamp(3.2,2rem+7vw,6.2rem) hero; --step-5 clamp(3.6,2rem+9vw,8rem) pull-quotes. Two-tone rule: headlines are --ink with EXACTLY the target words in --gold-ink, weight-matched (no italics, no size jump) — accent by color only. Tracking: display -0.02em, uppercase eyebrows +0.14em at --step--1. Line-height display 0.98, body 1.55. Max measure 62ch.
design_tokens.spacing_rhythm: 8pt base. Tokens --s1:8 --s2:16 --s3:24 --s4:40 --s5:64 --s6:96 --s7:144 --s8:200. Section vertical rhythm alternates tall/normal: anchor sections use --s8, connective sections --s6 — deliberate uneven cadence, not uniform. Global content max --wrap:1200px; a wider --bleed:min(1440px,96vw) for asset overspill; narrow --read:64ch for prose. Asymmetric gutters: left rail 1fr / content 8col / right rail 2fr swaps side per section to avoid grid monotony.
design_tokens.motion_easings_durations: Easings: --ease-out:cubic-bezier(.22,1,.36,1) (reveals), --ease-inout:cubic-bezier(.65,0,.35,1) (blends), --ease-spring:cubic-bezier(.34,1.56,.64,1) (mascot/CTA press only, small displacement). Durations: --t-fast:180ms, --t:420ms, --t-slow:720ms, --t-cine:1100ms (once, hero + own-it band). Reveal stagger step 60ms, capped at 6 children. Scroll-linked parallax runs on rAF, capped ±40px. prefers-reduced-motion: all durations→1ms, parallax→0, video posters swap in.

motion_system: Single global controller in reveal.js. (1) IntersectionObserver (threshold [0,.15,.5], rootMargin '-8% 0px -8%') adds .is-in to [data-reveal]; CSS transitions transform+opacity only (translateY(24px)/opacity0 → 0/1) with per-child --i index driving transition-delay:calc(var(--i)*60ms). (2) Section-to-section BLEND: each <section> exposes --sp (0→1 scroll progress through its own viewport window) written once per rAF frame from a throttled scroll loop over a small registered array of anchor sections; CSS consumes --sp for background cross-fade (paper→paper-2), mask-position, and parallax translate on decorative layers. Adjacent sections share edge color via a --sp-driven linear tint so the seam dissolves rather than cuts. (3) Performance rules: only transform/opacity/filter animate; decorative layers get will-change:transform + contain:layout paint; never animate width/top/box-shadow; videos are object-fit:cover, autoplay muted playsinline loop, paused via IO when off-screen (v.pause()) to save GPU. (4) Reduced-motion: matchMedia gate — observer still toggles .is-in but CSS zeroes transforms/delays, parallax loop never starts, all <video> replaced by their poster PNG. rAF loop is a single shared ticker (no per-element listeners); if >1 heavy asset visible, degrade parallax to step-quantized values.
anchor_moments: OWNS/RENTS hero toggle — the headline word 'RENTS' is a live crossout: DOM is OWNSRENTS; a rAF-driven --sp on the hero draws an SVG hand-strike line across 'RENTS' (stroke-dashoffset 1→0) while OWNS ink-fills gold; on hover/scroll the two words seesaw in weight. Mascot gestures (video) sit in the counter, a soft radial gold spotlight (single CSS radial, opacity via --sp) grounds it. Built: SVG stroke + CSS clip-path reveal on OWNS, JS only writes --sp.

Slop reveal wipe (Section 3) — a full-width split where a 'demo' still frame (FLUX, boxed screenshot mock) is torn away by a diagonal clip-path mask (--sp-driven polygon) to expose the same claim rendered as reproducible evals (mono text + tiny sparkline). No card; the tear IS the layout edge. Built: two stacked layers, top layer clip-path:polygon animated by --sp, drop-shadow on the tear seam; reduced-motion shows both halves side by side.

Own-it handover ledger (Section 4, inverted --ink band) — 'code / weights / evals' as three receipt lines that type-stamp in with a mono monospace tick and a checkmark drawn via SVG path length; a faint gold key-in-lock micro-loop (WAN webm) bleeds off the right edge. The dark band itself is the anchor contrast. Built: SVG stroke-dashoffset checks on .is-in, staggered; mono ledger uses tabular-nums.

Number-first savings dial (Section 6) — the visitor's typed inputs drive one BIG animated numeral (odometer via tween on rAF, tabular-nums) plus a single horizontal 'time reclaimed' bar that grows with --value; the honest-number motif (one number) is echoed visually. Built: JS clamps + tweens display value with requestAnimationFrame lerp; bar width via CSS var, no chart lib.

sections.name: Hero — Owns not Rents
sections.layout: Asymmetric 12-col: headline block cols 1–7 left-weighted, mascot well cols 7–12 overspilling the right --bleed. Eyebrow, H1 (2 lines, OWNS/RENTS accents), sub-line at --read width, CTA row.
sections.content: Eyebrow: 'THE ANTI-SLOP AI STUDIO'. H1: 'We build production AI your business actually OWNS — not RENTS.' Sub: 'Real systems, senior-built, measured against one honest number, handed over with the code, weights, and evals.' CTAs: 'Start a build' (solid gold), 'See what we ship' (ink outline).
sections.visual: assets/anim/bot.webm gold mascot, alpha, looping, floating idle; single radial gold spotlight behind it; SVG strike-line over RENTS.
sections.interaction: OWNS ink-fills gold + RENTS strike-through on load & re-emphasize on scroll (--sp); mascot parallax ±24px; CTA press uses --ease-spring scale .98.
sections.transition_in: Cinematic once: headline lines mask-reveal upward (--t-cine) staggered, mascot fades+rises, spotlight blooms.
sections.blend_with_neighbors: Bottom edge tints paper→paper-2 via --sp so it dissolves into Section 2; mascot's spotlight faint glow carries a few px past the seam.

sections.name: What we build — four owned systems
sections.layout: NOT a bento grid: a vertical editorial list of 4 rows, each row full-width with alternating side for the 3D object (left/right/left/right) and text on the opposite side; generous --s7 between rows.
sections.content: Sales agents, Automations, Multi-agent systems, Pipelines. Each: one blunt line + 2–3 word capability chips (mono). Section kicker: 'Four systems we build to OWN.'
sections.visual: Four transparent VP9-alpha loops: plane, gears, molecule, database — each bleeds off its outer margin, half in the gutter, slow float parallax.
sections.interaction: Each object plays only while its row is in view (IO pause/play); row text reveals with 60ms stagger; hovering a row lifts its object scale 1.03.
sections.transition_in: Rows reveal on .is-in translateY; objects fade+float from their bleed side.
sections.blend_with_neighbors: Paper-2 background; last row's object overspills toward Section 3's tear to hint the coming contrast.

sections.name: Fake it vs prove it
sections.layout: Full-bleed diagonal split. Left = 'slop' (dead demo screenshot, hatch-textured, desaturated), Right = 'proven' (reproducible evals, mono, sparkline). The diagonal tear is the divider.
sections.content: Pull-quote as typographic anchor (--step-5): 'Most AI you've been sold is slop: dead demos, rented wrappers, and accuracy nobody can reproduce.' Right side: reproducible eval snippet + 'one honest number' tag.
sections.visual: FLUX-generated fake 'demo' UI still (boxed, intentionally sterile) under a CSS hatch overlay on slop side; clean mono eval block on proven side.
sections.interaction: Scroll-driven clip-path tear (--sp) wipes slop away to reveal proven; sparkline draws via stroke-dashoffset.
sections.transition_in: Pull-quote words rise; tear seam sweeps as section centers.
sections.blend_with_neighbors: High-contrast beat; proven side's ink density leads into the inverted dark Section 4 — the tear's dark seam pre-echoes it.

sections.name: You own it — handover
sections.layout: INVERTED anchor band: full-width --ink background, --paper text, centered narrow --read column with a right-bleeding key-loop. Once-only dark section.
sections.content: H2 (gold-hi accents on 'own'): 'If you can't take the code, the weights, and the evals with you, you're renting theater.' Ledger: code / weights / evals — each a stamped handover line.
sections.visual: WAN-generated gold key-turning-in-lock micro-loop (alpha webm) bleeding off right edge; SVG checkmarks per ledger line.
sections.interaction: Ledger lines type-stamp + checkmark-draw staggered on .is-in; key loop plays in view only.
sections.transition_in: Band background wipes in from Section 3's dark seam (--sp continuity), text fades up.
sections.blend_with_neighbors: Bottom edge fades --ink→--paper so Section 5 returns to light without a hard cut.

sections.name: How a build runs — 6 steps
sections.layout: Horizontal senior-led path: a single connecting line (SVG) threading 6 numbered nodes; on narrow screens it stacks vertically. Uses existing kit stepper, restyled.
sections.content: 6 steps orbiting 'the one honest number': Scope the number → Senior design → Build in the open → Measure vs the number → Handover (code+weights+evals) → You run it. Blunt one-liners.
sections.visual: SVG path with a gold progress dash that fills as you scroll; small mono step indices; the number motif appears at center as a pinned figure.
sections.interaction: Scroll-linked path dash (--sp) fills; active node scales + gold dot; reduced-motion shows full path static.
sections.transition_in: Nodes reveal left→right stagger; path draws.
sections.blend_with_neighbors: Paper base; sets up the number theme that Section 6 pays off numerically.

sections.name: Savings calculator
sections.layout: Two-part asymmetric: left = compact input stack (labels + sliders/number fields), right = one dominant animated result numeral + a single time-reclaimed bar. No card chrome — inputs sit on paper with hairlines.
sections.content: Heading: 'See the number YOU could save.' Inputs are visitor's own: people affected, hours/week on the task, weeks/year. Output framed as THEIR reclaimed time (and a plain-language 'that's roughly X working weeks'). Zero prices, zero our-fees.
sections.visual: Big odometer numeral (tabular-nums, gold-ink) + growing gold bar; subtle mascot peeking (PNG pose) approving.
sections.interaction: Live rAF-tweened result on any input change; bar width via --value; debounced, keyboard-accessible sliders.
sections.transition_in: Inputs and numeral reveal; first value counts up from 0 once on entry.

sections.name: Free open-source tool
sections.layout: Editorial feature row: left prose + repo/link CTA, right a mono 'terminal' card (the ONLY mono-heavy block) showing an install/usage line and a live star/commit stamp.
sections.content: Pull-quote support: 'We build in the open.' One paragraph: what the tool is, why free, link 'Get it on GitHub'. Reinforces ownership ethos.
sections.visual: Mono terminal block (not glass — flat ink-on-paper inverted mini-panel), a small gears loop reused subtly.
sections.interaction: Copy-to-clipboard on the install line; typed-in caret animation on reveal (reduced-motion: static).
sections.blend_with_neighbors: Paper; hands off to the team/FAQ section.

sections.name: Small senior team + FAQ
sections.layout: Split: left = short 'senior-only' statement + tiny team row (2–4 monochrome portraits, no titles inflation), right = accordion FAQ (existing kit accordions restyled).
sections.content: Pull-quote: 'No offshore junior farms, no prompt-and-pray. Senior people, or we don't ship it.' FAQ: ownership, handover, the one number, timelines, what 'slop' means — blunt answers.
sections.visual: Duotone portraits (FLUX or real photos matted), gold hairline dividers; mascot small pose beside the statement.
sections.interaction: Accordion open/close (kit.js) with height transition + gold marker; portraits subtle grayscale→tint on hover.
sections.transition_in: Statement + portraits reveal; first FAQ item pre-opened.
sections.blend_with_neighbors: Paper-2 easing toward the contact close.

sections.name: Contact — tell us the number
sections.layout: Focused closing band: oversized headline left, existing #contactForm right in a clean hairline frame (no glass). Mascot gives a final wave (bot.webm variant).
sections.content: H2: 'Tell us the one number you need to hit.' Sub: 'We'll tell you if we can own it.' CTAs echo: 'Start a build'. Form fields: name, email, the number/goal, context.
sections.visual: bot.webm final pose bleeding bottom-left; a faint gold underline sweep beneath the headline.
sections.interaction: site.js form validation/submit unchanged; on success, mascot celebratory pose swaps; inline error states.
sections.transition_in: Headline underline sweeps (--sp), form fields reveal staggered.
sections.blend_with_neighbors: Final section; footer inherits paper, gold hairline top border ties back to hero accent.

savings_calculator_arch: Inputs (all visitor-supplied, no benchmarks fabricated): peopleAffected (int), hoursPerWeekOnTask (number), weeksPerYear (default 48, editable), and a single honest slider 'share of that task realistically automatable' (0–100%, visitor's own estimate, default 40%, clearly labeled 'your estimate'). Math is transparent and printed under the result: reclaimedHoursPerYear = people × hoursPerWeek × weeks × (share/100). Show as hours AND 'that's about X full-time working weeks' (÷40) — time only, never our price, never their dollars unless THEY optionally enter an hourly rate they own (kept optional, off by default, labeled 'optional — your rate'). UI: on 'input' event, debounce 40ms, recompute, then rAF-lerp the displayed numeral from current→target (tabular-nums odometer) and set --value on the bar (width:calc(var(--value)*1%)). No implied guarantee: persistent microcopy 'Your estimate, your numbers — not a promise. We measure the real number together during scoping.' Fully keyboard/AT accessible: sliders are <input type=range> with linked <input type=number>, aria-live='polite' on the result numeral, reduced-motion skips the count-up and sets final value directly.
visual_asset_plan: Hero radial spotlight — pure CSS radial-gradient (no asset). Blends: opacity driven by --sp behind mascot, bleeds a few px past section seam.

Mascot idle + wave + celebrate — reuse assets/anim/bot.webm; generate 1 extra WAN celebrate loop (alpha VP9) for Section 9 success + PNG poster fallbacks. Blends: alpha overspill into gutters, parallax, no box.

Four system objects — reuse existing plane/gears/molecule/database alpha webms in Section 2; if any missing, WAN 2.2 image-to-video from FLUX stills, rembg matte, ffmpeg VP9-alpha. Blends: half-in-gutter bleed, slow float parallax, side-alternating.

Section 3 'dead demo' still — FLUX: a deliberately sterile, generic SaaS dashboard mock (flat, lifeless) at ~1600px, exported PNG, then CSS hatch + desaturate overlay. Blends: lives under the diagonal clip-path tear as the 'slop' layer, torn away on scroll.
Section 3 sparkline + Section 5 path + Section 4 checkmarks — hand-authored inline SVG (no generation), stroke-dashoffset animated. Blends: drawn in place, gold stroke.
Section 4 key-in-lock micro-loop — WAN 2.2 from a FLUX gold key+lock still, alpha VP9 short loop, rembg+ffmpeg. Lives right-edge of dark band, bleeds off-canvas.
Section 8 portraits — real photos preferred; if placeholders needed, FLUX duotone gold/ink portraits, matted. Blends: hairline-framed row, grayscale→gold-tint on hover, no drop-shadow cards.

Textures — one subtle paper grain PNG (tileable, ~3% opacity) as body::before for warmth; CSS diagonal hatch pattern (repeating-linear-gradient) for slop side. Blends: global low-opacity, never a boxed tile.
css_js_architecture: Keep no-build static: plain <script src> + <link> in each HTML page, no bundler. LAYERING: (1) site.css = existing base, untouched except appending :root token additions guarded by comment block '/* dpr-2026 tokens */'. (2) assets/kit.css = existing components untouched. (3) NEW assets/dpr.css = all new section styles, scoped under section wrappers (.s-hero, .s-build, .s-slop, .s-own, .s-path, .s-calc, .s-oss, .s-team, .s-contact) so nothing leaks to other pages. BEM-lite naming: .block__el--mod, plus data-* hooks for JS (data-reveal, data-parallax, data-calc, data-sp). JS: keep existing site.js/kit.js loading; add assets/dpr.js as a single IIFE/module that: registers IO reveal, the shared rAF --sp ticker (only for sections with data-sp), video pause/play on visibility, calculator logic, clipboard, mascot pose swap. Guard everything behind matchMedia(prefers-reduced-motion) and feature-detects (IntersectionObserver, WebM alpha via canProbe). No new deps. Existing pages that don't include dpr.css/dpr.js are unaffected. Home page includes them last so cascade/order is predictable. Assets under assets/anim/ and assets/img/. Ship-safe: all JS null-checks its targets so partial pages don't throw.
distinctiveness: The structural spine is a single vertical 'ownership argument' that alternates rhythm and inverts ONCE: light editorial sections flow with side-swapping asymmetry (never a symmetric grid, never equal cards), building tension to one deliberate dark inverted band (Section 4 'You own it') that acts as the spine's fulcrum, then resolves back to light and pays off in the single big number (Section 6). Richness is concentrated in 4 scroll-authored anchor moments (OWNS/RENTS strike, slop tear, handover ledger, savings dial) rather than sprinkled uniformly. No repeated card component defines the page — each section has a distinct structural gesture (list-with-bleed, diagonal tear, inverted ledger, threaded path, input+numeral). That asymmetric-cadence + single-inversion + concentrated-anchors skeleton is what makes it a bespoke argument, not a bento template.
risks: VP9 alpha video unsupported (Safari) — mitigation: feature-detect, fall back to PNG poses/stills; never depend on video for meaning; all copy readable without video.
Scroll-linked --sp jank on low-end/GPU — mitigation: single shared rAF ticker, transform/opacity only, will-change+contain, cap parallax, quantize under load, IO-pause off-screen videos.
Two-tone gold failing contrast — mitigation: accented WORDS use --gold-ink (darker) on paper for AA; bright --gold reserved for fills/strokes/objects, verified with a contrast pass.

Calculator implying a guarantee or leaking our pricing — mitigation: time-first output, visitor-only inputs, persistent 'your estimate, not a promise' microcopy, optional rate off by default, no fees anywhere.
Reduced-motion users getting a broken/empty page — mitigation: .is-in still applies (content visible), CSS zeroes transforms, videos→posters, count-up→final value; test with the query forced on.

Regression on other pages from shared CSS/JS — mitigation: all new styles scoped under section classes, all JS null-guarded and only wired via data-* present on home; existing files appended-to, not rewritten.
Dark inverted band feeling like generic dark-glass — mitigation: flat --ink, no blur/glass, no glow cards; it's a typographic ledger, used exactly once.
build_order: Phase 0 — Tokens + scaffolding: add token block to site.css, create dpr.css/dpr.js shells, wire IO reveal + shared --sp ticker + reduced-motion guard. Gate: reveal works, no console errors, push.
Phase 1 — Hero anchor (Section 1): layout, OWNS/RENTS strike interaction, mascot, spotlight. Gate: critique_ui on hero-in-context ≥84 focal/type, live push.
Phase 2 — Sections 2 & 3: four-object bleed list + slop tear anchor. Gate: critique_ui ≥84 originality/structure, push.
Phase 3 — Sections 4 & 5: inverted handover ledger + threaded path. Gate: contrast + motion QA, critique_ui ≥84, push.
Phase 4 — Section 6 savings calculator: inputs, honest math, odometer, a11y, disclaimers. Gate: manual math check + AT check + critique_ui ≥84, push.
Phase 5 — Sections 7,8,9: OSS terminal, team+FAQ (kit accordions), contact form wiring. Gate: form submit works via site.js, critique_ui full-page ≥84, push.
Phase 6 — Asset pass: generate/replace WAN+FLUX assets, matte+VP9, posters, grain. Gate: cross-browser fallback check, Lighthouse perf, final critique_ui ≥84, push.
acceptance_84: Exactly one accent color used with disciplined two-tone headlines (accented words color-only, weight-matched).
No generic dark-glass cards and no equal-weight bento grid anywhere; each section has a structurally distinct gesture.
Clear focal hierarchy per section: one dominant element (headline or numeral), supporting elements demoted by size/color/space.
Mono type appears only in code/eval/terminal/ledger contexts, never in prose.
3–4 genuine high-craft anchor interactions present and functional (OWNS/RENTS, slop tear, handover ledger, savings dial).
Deliberate asymmetric spacing cadence with the single dark inversion as fulcrum — visible structural rhythm, not uniform tiling.
Motion is smooth (transform/opacity only, no jank), section seams blend rather than hard-cut.
Fully degrades: reduced-motion and no-WebM users see complete, legible, well-composed content.
Zero pricing/money anywhere except the visitor's-own-numbers savings calculator with explicit non-guarantee copy.
Type scale is fluid, consistent, and disciplined (limited steps, tight display leading, correct measure).
